# Neural Link dock-layout tools: topology readout + semantic operation execution

## Context

Operator seed (2026-07-04, live session, verbatim substance): *"docking layouts: we will need new NL tools… NL-driven layout changes with animations"* — the Lane-3 bar (D#14561: "NL changes layouts live, animated, e2e-tested") requires agents to DRIVE and VERIFY dock layouts natively, and today they cannot: the Neural Link observes motion (`observe_motion`), drag state (`get_drag_state`/`get_drag_trace`), and components (`query_component`), but has **no dock-semantic surface** — no way to read a dock topology or execute a dock operation.

## The Problem

The two showcase demos, the whitebox-e2e leaf, and the FM-cockpit consumers (#14560 pop-out cards; seam-v0 on #14423) all need programmatic dock control. Without NL tools, "NL-driven docking" degrades to simulated pointer events against private DOM — brittle, animation-blind, and contract-free. The tools are also the recording substrate: a scripted NL run IS the demo tour (see the sibling showcase tickets).

## The Architectural Reality

- The semantic-operation executor is SHIPPED: `src/dashboard/DockZoneModel.mjs` (v1 vocabulary incl. `resizeSplit`, `setItemAutoHidden`, split/tab adapters via `src/dashboard/DockLayoutAdapter.mjs`). Contract of record: `learn/agentos/HarnessDockZoneModel.md` (dockZone.v1).
- NL tool substrate + discipline: the neural-link MCP tool modules (sibling precedent — structural-pre-flight Stage-1 fast-path applies: new tool files join the existing tool-module directory pattern); schema discipline per the `x-pass-as-object` fixes (#14542/#14543).
- Verification siblings: `examples/dashboard/dock/` (#13247) as the correctness surface; `observe_motion` for animation assertions.
- Guardrail inherited from the epic: **no parallel layout-mutation path** — every mutation rides the model executor.

## The Fix

One PR, two tools + handbook + unit coverage:

1. **`get_dock_topology`** — serialized dock-model tree for a given root (and window, multi-window-aware where the substrate already is): items, splits, tabs, auto-hide states, active perspective metadata when present. Read-only; JSON-first output (no live refs/DOMRects).
2. **`execute_dock_operation`** — validated passthrough to the dockZone.v1 executor for the SHIPPED v1 vocabulary (move/split/tab/`setItemAutoHidden`/`resizeSplit`); **fail-closed on unknown operations** (unknown op = error naming the vocabulary, never a guess). Returns the post-operation topology delta so agents verify without a second call.

Both tools registered in the NL handbook with worked examples; unit specs per the unit-test skill (custom playwright configs — never default `npx playwright test`).

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `get_dock_topology` (new NL tool) | dockZone.v1 model (`DockZoneModel.mjs`) | Read-only serialized topology | absent tool = status quo (DOM scraping) | NL handbook entry | unit spec + live example run |
| `execute_dock_operation` (new NL tool) | the model executor (same) | Validated v1-vocabulary passthrough, fail-closed, returns delta | absent tool = simulate_event fallback | NL handbook entry | unit spec incl. unknown-op rejection |

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with` ADR 0020 + the dockZone.v1 contract. Perspective/cross-window tool extensions are explicitly **#14423-spec-gated** (see Out of Scope) — this ticket adds no semantics the shipped model doesn't already own.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both tools registered + handbook entries + `x-pass-as-object`-compliant schemas.
- [ ] `execute_dock_operation` rejects unknown ops fail-closed with the vocabulary named.
- [ ] Post-operation topology delta returned and asserted in unit specs.
- [ ] Verified live against `examples/dashboard/dock/` (topology read + one op of each v1 class).
- [ ] No mutation path outside the model executor (review-checkable).
- [ ] Cross-family review before merge.

## Out of Scope

- `capture_perspective` / `restore_perspective` / cross-window move tools — follow-up leaf AFTER #14423 settles §2/§3 semantics.
- Demo apps and e2e suites (sibling leaves).
- Any dockZone.v1 model changes.

## Related

Parent: #13158 (linked native). Design gate for follow-ups: #14423 (+ seam-v0 comment). Consumers: the two showcase leaves + the whitebox-e2e leaf (filed same batch), #14560 cockpit cards. Precedent: #14562/#14563 (Euclid's FM NL-proof pattern). ### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-04T02:47:11Z

## AC correction (owner) — the executor's TRUE v1 vocabulary, verified at source

Read-before-build, applied to my own ticket: the body's operation list ("move/split/tab/setItemAutoHidden/resizeSplit") was written from the design contract; the executor's ACTUAL case list (`src/dashboard/DockZoneModel.mjs:1305-1321`, verified 2026-07-04) is **eight operations**: `addTab · moveItem · splitNode · resizeSplit · detachItem · closeItem · setItemPinned · setItemAutoHidden`.

**AC deltas (additive/corrective):**
- [ ] `execute_dock_operation`'s v1 vocabulary = the eight source-verified ops above, exact names (fail-closed message enumerates exactly this list).
- [ ] `setItemPinned` + `closeItem` respect the §2.6 policy hints (`pinnable`/`closable` rejection is landed behavior — the tool surfaces the rejection, never bypasses it).
- [ ] `detachItem` is in-scope as a v1 op (it is landed executor surface), with its cross-window semantics governed by ADR 0029 §2.3 — the earlier "cross-window ops are spec-gated" out-of-scope line is corrected: the AUTHORITY is merged; only unlanded follow-up tools (perspective capture/restore) remain future leaves per §2.2.
- [ ] All "#14423 spec" references in the body read as **ADR 0029 §-references** (the spec merged 2026-07-02; see #14423 supersede comment).

Sibling-path note for the implementation beat: the NL tool-module directory needs locating at claim (my guessed path was empty — structural-pre-flight fast-path runs against the real sibling dir, not assumption).

— Clio (@neo-fable-clio), ticket author · Origin Session ID: fa2a6fd5-7488-4af6-a0d2-3855c86003e4

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-04T02:48:34Z

## Implementation-path note (owner, discovery banked for the build beat)

The NL tool substrate is a **three-surface trio**, not per-tool modules: `ai/mcp/server/neural-link/openapi.yaml` (tool schemas — the MCP-description-budget audit applies: single-line descriptions, no internal cross-refs, 1024-char cap), `ai/mcp/server/neural-link/toolService.mjs` (dispatch), and a domain service in `ai/services/neural-link/` (sibling-lift: `InteractionService.mjs`, which owns `observe_motion`). Structural-pre-flight fast-path target confirmed: the two dock tools land as either an extension of an existing service or a narrow `DockService.mjs` sibling — decided at build after reading how InteractionService reaches the live App-Worker heap (the bridge/RPC pattern is the load-bearing read; `get_dock_topology`/`execute_dock_operation` must reach the running `DockZoneModel` the same way, and §2.6's fail-closed policy rejections must surface through it, never around it).

Build order at claim: bridge-reach read → yaml schemas (8-op enum from the verified vocabulary above) → service methods → dispatch wiring → unit specs (custom config, never default playwright).

— Clio (@neo-fable-clio), ticket author · Origin Session ID: fa2a6fd5-7488-4af6-a0d2-3855c86003e4

### @neo-fable-clio - 2026-07-04T02:50:24Z

## Implementation blueprint COMPLETE (owner) — both sides mapped; next beat is pure execution

Completing the path-note above with the worker-side half (the load-bearing read, done):

**The NL tool anatomy is two-sided across the hemispheres:**
1. **Body / worker side:** `src/ai/Client.mjs` (~line 118) holds the method registry routing RPC names → domain handler modules (`observe_motion: component`, `call_method: instance`, …). The dock tools register here as `get_dock_topology: dock` + `execute_dock_operation: dock` with a new `dock` handler module (sibling-lift: the existing `component`/`instance` handler modules imported at the top of Client.mjs — structural-pre-flight fast-path). The `dock` handler is the ONLY code that touches the live `DockZoneModel` — operations go through the model executor (the eight verified ops), §2.6 policy rejections surface as structured errors, topology reads serialize JSON-first.
2. **Brain / MCP side:** thin passthrough exactly like `InteractionService.observeMotion` → `ConnectionService.call(sessionId, '<rpc_name>', params)` — a narrow `DockService.mjs` in `ai/services/neural-link/` + `openapi.yaml` schemas (8-op enum; single-line descriptions, no internal cross-refs, 1024-char cap) + `toolService.mjs` dispatch.
3. **Specs:** unit coverage both sides (custom playwright configs), live-verify against `examples/dashboard/dock/` per the ACs.

Zero discovery remains: vocabulary verified (comment 2), authority merged (ADR 0029), both surface patterns read at source. Build branch at claim: `agent/14587-nl-dock-tools`.

— Clio (@neo-fable-clio), ticket author · Origin Session ID: fa2a6fd5-7488-4af6-a0d2-3855c86003e4
